title: tabs/store: replace module-level mutable counters with crypto.randomUUID()
description: Fix ISS-008: tabs/store.ts uses module-level mutable `let tabCounter = 0; let paneCounter = 0;` (lines 58-59) to generate IDs. These counters do not reset during HMR, risking ID collisions. Replace with crypto.randomUUID() so IDs are unique without stateful counters.
action: In apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/tabs/store.ts: (1) Delete `let tabCounter = 0;` (line 58) and `let paneCounter = 0;` (line 59). (2) Rewrite `nextTabId()` (lines 61-63) to `return crypto.randomUUID();`. (3) Rewrite `nextPaneId()` (lines 65-67) to `return crypto.randomUUID();`. (4) Update the stale comment block (lines 56-57) `// Phase 4: 将计数器移入 zustand store 内部或使用 crypto.randomUUID() / // 模块级可变计数器在 HMR 时不重置，可能导致 ID 碰撞` to a single line documenting the crypto.randomUUID approach. crypto.randomUUID is available globally in Electron renderer (secure context).

criteria: grep -n 'crypto.randomUUID' apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/tabs/store.ts returns at least 2 matches (nextTabId + nextPaneId)
! grep -n 'let tabCounter = 0\|let paneCounter = 0' apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/tabs/store.ts returns no matches
cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck exits 0
convergence.verification: Run grep checks to confirm counters removed + crypto.randomUUID present, then `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` exits 0. crypto.randomUUID is typed in lib.dom.d.ts (included via tsconfig lib DOM), so no import needed.
convergence.definition_of_done: Tab and pane IDs are generated via crypto.randomUUID() — no module-level mutable state, no HMR collision risk, IDs remain unique string type (compatible with existing Tab.id/Pane.id string fields).

files.change: Delete `let tabCounter = 0;` and `let paneCounter = 0;`; rewrite `function nextTabId(): string { return crypto.randomUUID(); }` and `function nextPaneId(): string { return crypto.randomUUID(); }`; replace the 2-line Phase-4 comment with `// IDs generated via crypto.randomUUID() — no module-level mutable counters (HMR-safe).`

implementation: Step 1: Read store.ts to confirm counters at lines 58-59 and nextTabId/nextPaneId at lines 61-67, and that Tab.id/Pane.id are `string` (no format constraint on `tab-`/`pane-` prefixes).
Step 3: Rewrite nextTabId body from `return \`tab-${++tabCounter}-${Date.now()}\`;` to `return crypto.randomUUID();`. Same for nextPaneId.
Step 4: Replace the 2-line Phase-4 comment (lines 56-57) with the single-line HMR-safe comment.
Step 5: Run `cd apps/desktop && bun run typecheck` — crypto.randomUUID is globally available (DOM lib) so no import needed; expect exit 0.
Step 6: Grep-verify no `tabCounter`/`paneCounter` references remain anywhere in the file.

success_metrics: crypto.randomUUID used in both ID generators

reference.pattern: crypto.randomUUID() for unique identifiers in browser/Electron renderer contexts — replaces stateful counters; returns a v4 UUID string.

rationale.chosen_approach: crypto.randomUUID() over zustand-store-internal counters — UUIDs are globally unique (HMR-safe, no reset needed), require zero state, and return `string` matching the existing Tab.id/Pane.id types. The store-internal-counter alternative would still need reset logic on HMR.
decision_factors: Electron renderer is a secure context — crypto.randomUUID is available without polyfill.
Existing ID format `tab-${n}-${ts}` was only used as a unique string key; no code parses the prefix.
UUIDs are longer but string-key equivalence is preserved.
rationale.tradeoffs: UUIDs are less human-readable than `tab-1-1700000000` in devtools, but uniqueness/HMR-safety outweighs debug readability.

risks.risk: Some code parses tab/pane ID format expecting `tab-`/`pane-` prefix
risks.mitigation: grep `tab-\|pane-` across src/ for ID format dependencies before the change; if found, prefix the UUID (e.g. `\`tab-${crypto.randomUUID()}\``). Read_first already includes the store; a quick grep during implementation confirms safety.
